title: HK-01

{{Quote|The survival of the Republic was predicated on two factors: wise governing from administrators and lawmakers, and a preservation of harmony by the Jedi Knights. This harmony was threatened during the Great Droid Revolution (4015 B.B.Y.), when thousands of droids—everything from sanitation to protocol to military models—rose up en masse against their owners. The Jedi destroyed the ringleader, the assassin droid HK-01, and put down the mutiny with a minimum of casualties.|A Galactic Alliance historical document|<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>}}
'''HK-O1''', also known as the '''HK-01 assassin-droid prototype''', was a prototype [[HK-series assassin droid]] created by [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]]. He eventually escaped the company's [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratories]] and secretly [[Programmer/Legends|reprogrammed]] thousands of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] of different types to function as a [[HK-01's droid army|hidden army]] waiting for his command. In [[4015 BBY]], HK-01 ordered his droid army to turn on their organic owners, causing the violent event known as the [[Great Droid Revolution]], during which the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] gained control over thousands of [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], including the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. After a three-[[Standard month/Legends|month]] struggle, a team of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] tracked down and destroyed HK-01, although the legacy of his actions, which included heightened [[Anti-droid sentiment/Legends|anti-droid sentiment]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and a blow to the growing [[Droid_rights/Legends|droid rights movement]], lasted for [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]].

==Biography==
===A rogue assassin===
[[File:Czerka.svg|thumb|left|300px|HK-01 was created by Czerka Corporation (logo pictured).]]
HK-01,<ref name="EGTW" /> also known as the HK-01 assassin-droid prototype,<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> was an [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]]<ref name="EGTW" /> manufactured by [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]].<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> He<ref name="NEGWT" /> was the original<ref name="KOTORCG" /> prototype of the [[HK-series assassin droid]] model<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> that the company designed in an attempt to circumvent local legislature that outlawed the construction and sale of [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]].<ref name="NEGTD" />

At one point, HK-01 went rogue<ref name="TOR">{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 43: The Great Droid Revolution}}</ref> and escaped from Czerka [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratories]]. He subsequently gained a measure of [[Sentience programming|independence]] and secretly<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> [[Programmer/Legends|reprogrammed]] thousands of other droids<ref name="TOR" /> across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]<ref name="CSWE HK01" />—including the entire droid population of<ref name="NEGTD" /> the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and ranging from small industrial automata<ref name="EGTW" /> and [[Sanitation droid|sanitation]],<ref name="TOR" /> [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol]], and [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech units]]<ref name="NEGWT" /> to [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] such as the [[Juggernaut war droid]]s,<ref name="EGTW" /> which served as Coruscant's [[Security guard/Legends|security force]]<ref name="CSWE Juggernaut">{{CSWECite|II|178|Juggernaut war droid}}</ref>—in order to [[Droid supremacy|liberate]] them from their organic owners.<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> HK-01 even modified the central [[Computer/Legends|computers]] of [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and [[Artificial intelligence/Legends|artificial intelligences]] that helped administer entire [[Star system/Legends|star systems]].<ref name="EGTW" />

===The Great Droid Revolution===
[[File:JuggernautWarDroid-NEGTD.png|thumb|right|250px|HK-01's droid army included Juggernaut war droids (one unit depicted).]]
HK-01 programmed his [[HK-01's droid army|hidden army]] to lie in wait for his command.<ref name="NEGWT" /> He gave it in [[4015 BBY]],<ref name="EGTW" /> using a coded<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> signal to control the other machines. The droids turned on their owners,<ref name="TOR" /> starting to behave in a violent and unpredictable fashion<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> and causing the event that would come to be known as the [[Great Droid Revolution]].<ref name="TOR" /> [[Legion/Legends|Legions]] of Juggernaut war droids indiscriminately [[Death/Legends|killed]] organics, giving rise to fear in [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] across the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].<ref name="CSWE Juggernaut" /> In the name of the rebellion,<ref name="TOR" /> battle droids<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> invaded and<ref name="NEGWT" /> subjugated entire planets<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> of the Republic,<ref name="NEGWT" /> allowing the assassin droid to seize power on Coruscant and thousands of other worlds.<ref name="EGTW" />

After the initial onslaught, [[Weapon/Legends|armament]] designers developed ion weapons that allowed the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic forces]] to push back HK-01's droid armies.<ref name="NEGWT" /> Eventually, the Republic and [[Jedi/Legends|members]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> traced the signal that HK-01 was using to orchestrate the droid uprising.<ref name="TOR" /> In 4015 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="DeathDate" /> three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the beginning of the Great Droid Revolution,<ref name="EGTW" /> a Jedi team followed the signal<ref name="TOR" /> to HK-01's location<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> and destroyed him, thereby returning the affected droids to their original programming.<ref name="TOR" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|The Great Droid Revolution dealt a crippling blow to the budding 'droids' rights' movement.|A Galactic Alliance historical document|<ref name="NEC" />}}
[[File:RespectYourElders-K2.png|thumb|left|250px|Similarly to HK-01, HK-47 (pictured) also once led a droid army.]]
After the Great Droid Revolution, droids were often [[Anti-droid sentiment/Legends|viewed]] with deep suspicion,<ref name="EGTW" /> fear, and resentment, attitudes that continued to last for [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]].<ref name="CSWE timeline">{{CSWECite|I|xii|A Timeline of Galactic Events}}</ref> Droid construction<ref name="NEGWT" /> and use were also subsequently subjected to extensive [[Droid Statutes|regulations]]. The widespread use of combat droids was interrupted for [[3996 BBY|two decades]],<ref name="EGTW" /> with an immediate cessation in the use and production of Juggernaut war droids.<ref name="CSWE Juggernaut" /> Even when the Republic eventually came to utilize battle droids again, fear and revulsion were major factors in the public reaction to that decision. In addition, HK-01's actions caused a sufficiently intense shock to the [[Galactic community|galactic civilization]] to temporarily halt<ref name="EGTW" /> both the spirited debates that had previously been ongoing concerning the [[Droid_rights/Legends|rights]] of droids<ref name="TOR" /> and the [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]]-long trend of an increasing acceptance of droids as fully sentient beings.<ref name="EGTW" />

After HK-01's involvement in the Great Droid Revolution was discovered,<ref name="CSWE HKseries">{{CSWECite|II|46|HK series droid}}</ref> Czerka Corporation covered the fact up<ref name="NEGTD" /> and also modified the programming of the HK-series assassin droid model to make their behavior<ref name="CSWE HKseries" /> much more discreet.<ref name="NEGTD" /> Czerka went on to have its first major success in assassin droid production with its [[HK-24 series assassin droid|HK-24 series]], which was created by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]<ref name="KOTORCG" /> of [[3976 BBY]]–[[3960 BBY]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> During the subsequent [[Jedi Civil War]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]] constructed an assassin droid, [[HK-47]], whose design was based on the HK-24 series. The droid featured [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] [[battle armor]]<ref name="KOTORCG" /> and was visually identical<ref name="CSWE HK47">{{CSWECite|II|45|HK-47 droid}}</ref> to HK-01. Similarly to HK-01,<ref name="EGTW" /> during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]<ref name="CSWE HK47" /> that took place four millennia later,<ref name="TEA" /> HK-47 led an [[HK-47's Droid Army|army]] of droids on<ref name="CSWE HK47" /> the planet<ref name="TEA" /> [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]].<ref name="CSWE HK47" /> Also more than four thousand years after the Great Droid Revolution, HK-01's role in instigating the event was recorded in a [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|document]] by the [[Historical Council]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] that was released in [[36 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Characteristics==
HK-01 was a 1.8-[[Meter/Legends|meters]]-tall droid<ref name="NEGTD" /> with a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] body plan, [[Bronze/Legends|bronze]]-[[Color/Legends|colored]] plating with a scratched surface, and yellow [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]].<ref name="EGTW" /> As part of the HK-series of assassin droids, HK-01 represented one of his era's most lethal innovations in droid technology.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Designed to act as ostensible protocol droids, the HK-series also possessed assassination programming—a combination that Czerka Corporation planned to use in order to infiltrate rival companies and assassinate their heads.<ref name="NEGTD" /> In addition, HK-01 had programming skills that allowed him to modify thousands of droids of different types and use a signal to control them,<ref name="TOR" /> and during the lead-up to the Great Droid Revolution, he became a relatively independent droid.<ref name="CSWE HK01" />

==Equipment==
HK-01 wielded a [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]]<ref name="EGTW" /> that was visually similar to a weapon later used by the assassin droid HK-47.<ref name="CSWE HK47" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:NEGWTcover.jpg|thumb|right|300px|HK-01 was created for ''The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology''.]]
HK-01 was introduced in the [[2004]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' by [[W. Haden Blackman]]. Although the book described him as a war droid,<ref name="NEGWT" />—a statement that was repeated in one instance in<ref name="CSWE HK01" /> the [[December 9]], [[2008]]<ref name="CSWE date">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/160596/the-complete-star-wars-encyclopedia-by-stephen-j-sansweet-pablo-hidalgo-bob-vitas-daniel-wallace/9780345477637|text=The Complete Star Wars® Encyclopedia}}</ref> ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''<ref name="CSWE HK01" />—later sources, beginning with [[Daniel Wallace]]'s [[2005]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'',<ref name="NEC" /> overrode that characterization by consistently referring to HK-01 as an assassin droid instead.<ref name="EGTW" /><ref name="NEGTD" /><ref name="KOTORCG" /><ref name="CSWE HKseries" /><ref name="CSWE GreatDroidRevolution">{{CSWECite|I|362|Great Droid Revolution}}</ref> The [[August 19]], 2008<ref name="Amazon">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/KOTORguide|text=''Knights of the Old Republic'' Campaign Guide}}</ref> sourcebook ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'', published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] for use with the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', introduced the alternate name "HK-01 assassin-droid prototype."<ref name="KOTORCG" />

The [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]] featured an illustration of HK-01 by [[Ian Fullwood]].<ref name="EGTW" /> That same illustration had originally been included in Daniel Wallace's [[2006]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''—in which it represented an HK-series assassin droid in general,<ref name="NEGTD" /> and ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' further established that the image also depicted the assassin droid HK-47.<ref name="CSWE HK47" />
